_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Research and evaluate available technologies and standards to meet customer requirements.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting data consumer discovery, translating consumer data needs into data product requirements with defined quality, freshness, and access standards.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI DQ domain. DQ-S-007 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-A-003 (TF, cosine: 0.536), OP-S-003 (OP, cosine: 0.527).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.4/10 (raw 0.644). Shared vocabulary: [requirements, standards]. DCWF-only terms [available, customer, evaluate, meet, research, technologies] vs WIDAI-only terms [access, conducting, consumer, data, defined, discovery] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.870 (P=0.883, R=0.857). P exceeds R by 0.025 — the DCWF element's 11 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 21 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Research and evaluate available technologies and standards to meet customer requ" and "Skill in conducting data consumer discovery, translating consumer data needs int" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.583 < STS 0.644 (gap -0.061). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [available, customer, evaluate] and [access, conducting, consumer] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 12.0%. Shared tokens: [requirements, standards]. DCWF-only: [available, customer, evaluate, meet, research, technologies]. WIDAI-only: [access, conducting, consumer, data, defined, discovery].
